commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh You have turned for me my mourning into dancing: you have put off my sackcloth, and girded me with gladness; Augustine of Hippo 12. "Thou hast turned My mourning into joy to Me" (ver. Whom I, the Church, having received, the First-Begotten from the dead, now in the dedication of Thine house, say, "Thou hast turned my mourning into joy to me. Thou hast put off my sackcloth, and girded me with gladness. " Thou hast torn off the veil of my sins, the sadness of my mortality; and hast girded me with the first robe, with immortal gladness.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Joy. When thou orderest the angel, 2 Kings xxiv. 16. (Calmet)
Sackcloth, of human nature, which was cut, and the price of our redemption came forth. (St. Augustine; St. Jerome)
Thou hast changed my mourning weeds for robes of joy. (Du Hamel)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
